title: Optimize p99, not average latency
description: Tune and report tail latency under realistic concurrency, because the slowest requests define the user experience and SLO.
body: Use when measuring or tuning serving performance. Do not report mean latency from a single-request benchmark — it hides queueing, batching stalls, and GC/cold-path spikes that dominate the tail. Actions: load-test at realistic concurrency, measure p95/p99/p99.9, and tune batching/queue limits against the tail. Validation signals include a p99 under the SLO at target QPS and a latency histogram, not just an average. Risk boundary: a system that looks fast on average can violate the SLO for the slowest 1% of users; require tail measurement under load before declaring it ready.
